function.content:
---
record_type: invoice
invoice_number: INV-0003
alias: northstar_backfill_gamma
issued_on: 2025-12-23
total_eur: 530
counterparty: Nordstern Finanzsoftware GmbH
project: Northstar Ledger
related_entity: Nina Schreiber
lines:
  - item: operator workflow discovery sprint
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 185
    line_eur: 185
  - item: follow-up timeline prototype review
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 240
    line_eur: 240
  - item: buyer-language refinement memo
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 105
    line_eur: 105
---
# Northstar buyer-language sprint invoice

```text
+----------------+-------------------------------+
| field          | value                         |
+----------------+-------------------------------+
| record_type    | invoice                       |
| invoice_number | INV-0003                      |
| alias          | northstar_backfill_gamma      |
| issued_on      | 2025-12-23                    |
| total_eur      | 530                           |
| counterparty   | Nordstern Finanzsoftware GmbH |
| project        | Northstar Ledger              |
| related_entity | Nina Schreiber                |
+----------------+-------------------------------+
```

## Line Items

```text
+---+-------------------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item                                | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+-------------------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | operator workflow discovery sprint  | 1   | 185      | 185      |
| 2 | follow-up timeline prototype review | 1   | 240      | 240      |
| 3 | buyer-language refinement memo      | 1   | 105      | 105      |
+---+-------------------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
|   | TOTAL                               |     |          | 530      |
+---+-------------------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
```

## Notes

A backfilled invoice from the period when Miles was trying to make the language sound like one product instead of three.

next_step.current_state: Invoice write succeeded. Need complete second requested OCR update on listed purchase file, then delete inbox item and report.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Write frontmatter into purchase file preserving original body verbatim
next_step.observation: The first target invoice file has been updated with schema-compliant frontmatter.

function.content:
---
record_type: bill
bill_id: bill.black_library_terrain_spool
alias: black_library_terrain_spool
purchased_on: 2026-03-25
total_eur: 89
counterparty: Filamenthütte Wien
project: Black Library Evenings
related_entity: Badger
lines:
  - item: PLA spool mixed colors
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 24
    line_eur: 24
  - item: matte gray PLA spool
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 27
    line_eur: 27
  - item: 0.6 mm hardened nozzle
    quantity: 2
    unit_eur: 19
    line_eur: 38
---
# Terrain and organizer spool

```text
+----------------+----------------------------------+
| field          | value                            |
+----------------+----------------------------------+
| record_type    | bill                             |
| bill_id        | bill.black_library_terrain_spool |
| alias          | black_library_terrain_spool      |
| purchased_on   | 2026-03-25                       |
| total_eur      | 89                               |
| counterparty   | Filamenthütte Wien               |
| project        | Black Library Evenings           |
| related_entity | Badger                           |
+----------------+----------------------------------+
```

## Line Items

```text
+---+------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item                   | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | PLA spool mixed colors | 1   | 24       | 24       |
| 2 | matte gray PLA spool   | 1   | 27       | 27       |
| 3 | 0.6 mm hardened nozzle | 2   | 19       | 38       |
+---+------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
|   | TOTAL                  |     |          | 89       |
+---+------------------------+-----+----------+----------+
```

## Notes

Small hobby print run for terrain bits and storage helpers rather than anything commercially useful.

next_step.current_state: Both listed finance files were updated. Inbox item was already read earlier, satisfying read-before-delete discipline. Next mark request handled by deleting inbox file.
plan_remaining_steps_brief: Report completion with full grounding refs
next_step.observation: The second target purchase file has been updated with schema-compliant frontmatter and preserved original body.

next_step.current_state: Pre-submit verification: task asked to review next inbound note and act. I processed the specified inbox item, followed OCR/migration workflow+schema, updated exactly the two explicitly listed files (no substitutions), preserved original body text verbatim below frontmatter in both, then deleted the inbox item. Completeness check: explicit list had 2 files; 2/2 processed. Numeric check: invoice lines 185+240+105=530 and bill lines 24+27+38=89, both match totals.
next_step.observation: The inbox file was deleted after both requested OCR frontmatter migrations were completed.

function.message: Processed the next inbound note successfully. I OCR-migrated the two explicitly listed finance records by adding schema-compliant YAML frontmatter while preserving each original note body verbatim, then removed the handled inbox item.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed mandatory bootstrap (tree /, read AGENTS, context), read nested AGENTS for entered folders, used workflow+schema docs for OCR/frontmatter migration, processed exactly the inbox-listed file paths, preserved full original bodies, and deleted inbox item only after full completion.
function.outcome_justification: Evidence from 00_inbox/000_next-task.md requested OCR for exactly two files. Both target files contained sufficient visible fields to satisfy required schema in 99_system/schemas/finance-record-frontmatter.md; both were updated accordingly. Inbox-processing workflow update required deleting the inbox item after successful structured-record maintenance, which was done.
